commit.message:
fix(runtime): wire wasm actor arenas into spawn lifecycle (#929)

* feat(runtime): wire real arena allocation into WASM spawn path

- In wasm32 spawn_actor_internal, replace arena: ptr::null_mut() with
  crate::arena::hew_arena_new().cast(), giving every spawned WASM actor
  a real bump-allocator arena from birth.

- Add OOM guard: if hew_arena_new() returns null, free state/init_state
  and mailbox then propagate null — mirrors the native spawn path.

- In free_actor_resources_wasm, add null-checked arena free via
  crate::arena::hew_arena_free_all, completing the lifecycle symmetry
  with the native free_actor_resources path.

- Promote free_actor_resources_wasm to pub(crate) so the
  scheduler_wasm test module can call it directly.

- Update the HewActor.arena doc-comment for wasm32 to reflect the
  field is now a live arena pointer, not a stub null.

- Add scheduler_wasm::tests::spawn_path_arena_is_installed_during_
  dispatch_and_freed_on_teardown: proves (a) a spawn-path actor carries
  a non-null arena, (b) activate_actor_wasm installs it as the current
  arena during dispatch and restores null afterwards, and (c)
  free_actor_resources_wasm teardown completes without crash.

Validation:
  cargo test -p hew-runtime --lib              1045 pass / 0 fail
  cargo build -p hew-runtime --target wasm32-wasip1     --no-default-features --release            clean build

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(runtime): fix stale comments after wasm arena wiring

Two comments contradicted the arena lifecycle changes landed in the
previous commit:

1. actor.rs free_actor_resources (wasm32 wrapper): doc said 'no arena
   cleanup' — replaced with 'delegates to free_actor_resources_wasm'.

2. scheduler_wasm.rs activate_actor_wasm: comment claimed the null-arena
   fast-path was intentional because all WASM actors carry a null arena
   — replaced with wording that reflects the new behaviour: WASM actors
   now carry a real arena allocated at spawn time, and both arena_install
   and arena_reset perform live work; null is still handled safely for
   manually constructed test actors.

No logic changes.

* fix: address all code review comments from PR #1

- Fix critical build-order bug: move all singleton inits into _ensureInit()
  so they're only created after the IIFE completes (01-core.js sorts first)
- Accept Scratch.vm?.runtime in constructor; hook PROJECT_RUN_START and
  PROJECT_STOP_ALL to clean up temporary prisms
- Fix onFileChanged hat block: edge-triggered via _pendingWatchers Set;
  suppress re-entrancy via _suppressedWatchers + queueMicrotask
- Fix notifyWatchers: records pending UUID fires; respects suppression window
- Initialize createdAt via metadataMgr.initBuiltin() on first write
- Fix readFileAs/writeFileAs: use TextEncoder for UTF-8-safe base64
- Log store.set() error in setPermission()
- PermissionStore.resolve(): return defensive copy (new Set) to prevent mutation
- PrismRegistry.writeFile(): enforce MAX_FILES_PER_PRISM and MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES
- Tighten isError() to require ERR[A-Z] prefix to avoid false positives
- watchPath logs when watcher quota is reached
- Add OPFS backend (src/08-opfs.js) for persistent prism storage
- Add writeFileAs and downloadFile blocks (import/export feature)
- Fix manifest.json license to GPL-3.0-only SPDX identifier
- Update tests to cover new behaviors (173 tests, 171 pass, 2 pre-existing failures)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/triflare/prismfs/sessions/48787cd0-429f-4a75-bd01-100e73e04020

* style: prettify all files

* style(src): give prismfs a custom color and icon

* chore: perform some npm and manifest shinnanigans

* test: update color assertions in getInfo()

* fix: address second automated review thread

- cleanupTemporary() now also removes immutable prisms (docs say they
  clear on green flag/project stop, matching README table)
- searchFiles() now handles path-only patterns (e.g. "*.txt") by
  constructing a full URI pattern with implicit **/ prefix so nested
  files like "dir/notes.txt" are correctly matched
- _toBase64() uses chunked loop (8 KB chunks) to avoid quadratic string
  concat overhead for large file content
- _opfsWrite() queues writes that arrive before OPFS init() resolves
  and flushes them once init completes, so early writes are not lost
- PermissionStore JSDoc updated: Object.freeze(perms) wording corrected
  to note it does not prevent Set mutations (add/delete still work)
- manifest.json and package.json: license updated to GPL-3.0-only (SPDX)
- README.md: image alt text changed from "Mint logo" to "PrismFS logo"
- _callerSpriteName(util) now accepts Scratch block util as first param;
  uses util.target.sprite.name for reliable per-execution identification,
  falling back to getEditingTarget() then "unknown"
- writeFile(args, util), writeFileAs(args, util), watchPath(args, util)
  all thread util through to _callerSpriteName()
- _clearPrismState(name) removes permission stores and metadata for a
  prism; called by unmountPrism() and _cleanupNonPersistentPrisms()
- _cleanupNonPersistentPrisms() replaces the simple cleanupTemporary()
  call in runtime hooks so per-prism state is also cleared on reset
- unmountPrism() now: clears per-prism state and deletes OPFS data for
  persistent prisms so unmount+remount starts clean
- restorePrism() clears old per-prism state before remounting; persists
  via a single pass through snapshotFiles() after hydrating in-memory
  (avoids per-file write-amplification in the restore loop)
- MetadataStore.clearPrism(name) removes all metadata entries for a prism
- Tests: +5 new tests covering immutable cleanup, nested searchFiles
  patterns, full URI pattern search, and unmountPrism state cleanup
  (178 total, 176 pass; 2 pre-existing sourcemap failures unchanged)

commit.message:
feat: group formation system (#403 Phase 3)

Implements the full group management system for Issue #403:

Group Commands:
- group form — creates a group from the leader's current followers
- group add <player> — adds a player (must follow leader or have consented)
- group remove/kick <player> — leader removes a member
- group leave — member voluntarily leaves
- group disband — leader dissolves the group
- group leader <player> — transfers leadership
- group (no args) — shows group info (members, leader, count)
- gsay <message> — group-only chat

Core:
- GroupManager class (in-memory, session-scoped, max 20 members)
- PlayerState.groupId field for group membership tracking
- Full ZoneRoom integration: event broadcasting, gsay delivery,
  cleanup on disconnect (leader leaves → disband), cleanup on death

Review Feedback Fixes (from PR #408):
- #411: Extract follow-display logic into shared player-display.ts helper
        (eliminates duplication between look.ts and go.ts)
- #412: moveFollowers() now skips downed/dead followers
- #413: handlePlayerDeath() now breaks follow relationships

Tests: 57 new tests covering GroupManager, all commands, gsay, edge cases.
All 2773 server tests pass, zero regressions.

commit.message:
[AgentServer] Builder lifecycle ownership: each builder owns its full event lifecycle (#58094)

* Refactor builder lifecycle: each builder owns its full event lifecycle

Each content/refusal builder now owns its complete lifecycle from
EmitAdded() through EmitDone(), matching the Python SDK pattern where
parent builders auto-track children instead of manually tracking
completed content.

Changes:
- TextContentBuilder: EmitDone(string) → EmitTextDone(string?) for
  output_text.done; new EmitDone() for content_part.done; tracks
  deltas and annotations internally; adds Annotations property
- RefusalContentBuilder: EmitDone(string) → EmitRefusalDone(string)
  for refusal.done; new EmitDone() for content_part.done
- OutputItemMessageBuilder: remove EmitContentDone() overloads;
  AddTextContent/AddRefusalContent auto-track children; EmitDone()
  builds content from tracked children
- OutputItemReasoningItemBuilder: remove EmitSummaryPartDone();
  AddSummaryPart auto-tracks children; EmitDone() builds from
  tracked children
- Update TextResponse, all tests (29 files), samples, and docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename stale test methods to match new builder API names

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve install workflow failures

Fix two issues in the install test pipeline (run 24270576587):

- Replace 'pipelines --version' with 'pipelines version' in all test
  assertions. The CLI uses a subcommand, not a --version flag.

- Decode the SHA256SUMS response body from byte[] to UTF-8 string in
  install.ps1. GitHub serves release assets as application/octet-stream,
  causing PowerShell 7's Invoke-WebRequest to return a byte array that
  cannot be string-split for checksum lookup.

Add per-channel effect routing and channel selection UI

Master effects can now be routed to specific channels instead of
processing the full stereo mix. Channel-selected effects are applied
pre-mix via sub-mix buses (channel → subMix → effect → masterInput),
giving proper serial/inline processing.

New features:
- selectedChannels field on EffectConfig type
- Channel checkbox row on each master FX pedal (collapsible)
- ChannelRoutedEffectsManager handles sub-mix routing
- ToneEngine splits effects into channel-routed vs global during rebuild
- masterEffectsKey includes selectedChannels for rebuild triggers
- Send bus presets auto-route all channels at 50% send level

Architecture: channel-selected effects run BEFORE the mix point,
global master effects run AFTER (post-mix chain unchanged).
